danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies bearing Sigma's emblem; specific boss character designs
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates threat to world peace; Mavericks are destructive Reploids that threaten humanity

danger_signals.visual_form: Three powerful Mavericks (Serges, Agile, Violen) with distinct character designs; Zero's severed body parts
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to X; signals organized rebellion and tactical danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles, traps, and concentrated enemy placements within eight Maverick-controlled territories
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat zones requiring caution and skill navigation
Indicates Sigma's continued influence despite apparent defeat; signals systemic threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Base location where Dr. Cain and command operate
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone and mission control center; allied territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items, armor pieces, and special weapons from defeated bosses
safety_signals.meaning: Progression indicators; growing capability and readiness

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible pieces of Zero scattered among eight Maverick locations
safety_signals.meaning: Completing collection enables Zero's reactivation, which strengthens X's position

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Likely institutional insignia (not detailed in sources)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eight distinct Maverick boss characters with thematic designs (Wire Sponge, Morph Moth, Flame Stag, Crystal Snail, Overdrive Ostrich, Bubble Crab, Wheel Gator, Magna Centipede)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Eight separate geographic territories directly south of North Pole where Mavericks operate

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Trio designation; Zero's body parts as symbolic leverage
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Three unique bosses (Serges, Agile, Violen) with distinct combat characteristics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: X-Hunter fortification in North Pole; drift among eight Maverick locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears as final boss; revealed as computer virus entity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Central Computer location (one of eight visited stages); manufactured Maverick at factory bearing Sigma emblem

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Sigma conflict (6 months after first game)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Apparent peace with continued Maverick activity; X promoted to Maverick Hunters head
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: X assigned to exterminate eight Maverick leaders; X-Hunters emerge and taunt with Zero's body parts

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Continued Maverick rebellion despite Sigma's death
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight distinct Maverick-controlled territories; manufactured Mavericks bearing Sigma's emblem in factories
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: X launches full assault on Reploid factory; Mavericks threaten daily life; X-Hunters gain control of North Pole

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: X-Hunter fortification as final stage location; Serges, Agile, and Violen holding Zero's body parts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: X-Hunters drift among eight Maverick locations, luring X with Zero's parts as bait

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Conditional ending based on Zero's parts collection
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: If parts collected: gray-armored Zero clone appears with Sigma; if not collected: X-Hunters have stolen parts from headquarters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player choice creates branching narrative outcomes affecting final boss composition and ending

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sigma retreats; demonstrates non-corporeal existence; facility self-destructs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zero creates escape passage; Zero questions allegiance with humans (narrative ambiguity); X and Zero evacuate and watch destruction

environmental_storytelling.example: Eight distinct geographic zones south of North Pole, each controlled by a thematically-named Maverick boss (Wire Sponge, Wheel Gator, etc.), implying territorial organization and resource control
Zero's dismembered body parts scattered across eight locations create a collect-them-all meta-narrative that changes ending outcome, visually representing X's struggle to restore his fallen partner
X's opening mission to assault a Reploid factory bearing Sigma's emblem communicates industrial-scale threat and suggests Sigma's ability to manufacture Mavericks despite his apparent defeat

environmental_storytelling.technique: North Pole fortress as final stronghold
environmental_storytelling.example: X-Hunters' geographic isolation in North Pole signals a fortified, organized enemy structure requiring X's final push

environmental_storytelling.technique: Central Computer as series climax location
environmental_storytelling.example: One of the eight visited stages is revealed as Central Computer containing Sigma, creating circular narrative structure where earlier exploration directly enables final confrontation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Promoted to Maverick Hunters head; launches full assaults on Maverick strongholds; pursues X-Hunters relentlessly; capable of collecting Zero's parts across eight territories

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Apparent death (first game); secretly orchestrating from computer network
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Reveals himself after X-Hunters defeated; demonstrates ability to control manufactured Mavericks remotely; retreats when cornered; taunts X with prophecy of return as virus
Body parts collected/held by X-Hunters as leverage; if reassembled, fights alongside X; questions humanity allegiance in final encounter; creates escape passage suggesting agency despite recent death

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Active antagonists, mid-hierarchy between Mavericks and Sigma
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Drift among eight Maverick locations; initiate psychological warfare by taunting X with Zero's body; each holds a body part and engages X individually; defeated before Sigma reveals true role

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Distributed across territories, seemingly independent but Sigma-aligned
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Occupy distinct geographic zones; engage X in themed combat scenarios; defeated to acquire special weapons; serve as intermediate obstacles before X-Hunters confrontation
Indicate industrial-scale threat; trigger X's factory assault; demonstrate Sigma's persistent influence in production systems

notes: Mega Man X2 communicates world-state through hierarchical threat structure: generic Mavericks < X-Hunters < Sigma (ultimate antagonist). The game uses geographic isolation (eight territories + North Pole fortress + Central Computer) to visualize power distribution. Zero's body parts function as both literal narrative objects and symbolic communication of stakes. The conditional ending based on collection creates branching manifestation of world-state consequences. Sigma's final revelation as computer virus (rather than physical entity) suggests infrastructure-level threat requiring different conceptual understanding. The game's visual communication relies heavily on boss character design differentiation and stage-specific environmental theming rather than explicit UI elements.
sources_used: tcrf - Mega Man X2 - The Cutting Room Floor (for plot, Maverick names, X-Hunter details, unused content context)
wikipedia - Mega Man X2 (for plot details, world-state descriptions, character information, game mechanics)
